message:
fix(efficiency,assortativity): kwarg-name + missing-kwarg parity (br-effkw, br-assortkw, br-copyedgeord queued)

Round 17 Cycle 3 — less-visited-area audit:

[MEDIUM] br-assortkw: fnx.degree_assortativity_coefficient was a
direct re-export of the Rust binding which only accepts (g). nx's
surface is (G, x='out', y='in', weight=None, nodes=None). Users
passing weight= or x/y= TypeError'd. Added a Python wrapper that
takes the full signature; keeps the Rust fast-path for default
parameters, delegates to nx otherwise. Verified weighted /
in-vs-out / nodes-filter all match nx.

[LOW] br-effkw: efficiency / global_efficiency / local_efficiency
named first param 'g' (lowercase) where nx uses 'G'. Kwarg-style
drop-in calls TypeError'd. Python wrappers with G as first param.

[MEDIUM] br-copyedgeord — QUEUED, Rust-side: Graph(fnx_src) and
G.copy(as_view=False) and G.to_undirected(as_view=False) sometimes
emit edges with endpoints reversed (second edge of
path_graph(3).copy() comes out as (2,1) instead of (1,2)). Needs a
Rust fix in the constructor/copy paths; beyond this round's scope.
Currently triggers 3 pre-existing-but-latent test failures
(test_graph_from_graph_unchanged, test_copy_as_view_like_networkx,
test_to_undirected_as_view_like_networkx).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -2909,6 +2909,26 @@ def local_edge_connectivity(
 )
 
 
+def degree_assortativity_coefficient(G, x="out", y="in", weight=None, nodes=None):
+    """Compute degree assortativity of graph.
+
+    br-assortkw: accepts nx's full parameter surface (``x``, ``y``,
+    ``weight``, ``nodes``). The Rust fast-path only honours the
+    default (unweighted, all-nodes, out-in for directed) — delegate
+    to nx whenever any kwarg is non-default.
+    """
+    if x == "out" and y == "in" and weight is None and nodes is None:
+        return _raw_degree_assortativity_coefficient(G)
+    return _call_networkx_for_parity(
+        "degree_assortativity_coefficient",
+        G,
+        x=x,
+        y=y,
+        weight=weight,
+        nodes=nodes,
+    )
+
+
 def average_neighbor_degree(
     G,
     source="out",
@@ -3903,11 +3923,26 @@ def all_simple_paths(G, source, target, cutoff=None):
 
 # Algorithm functions — efficiency
 from franken_networkx._fnx import (
-    efficiency,
-    global_efficiency,
-    local_efficiency,
+    efficiency as _raw_efficiency,
+    global_efficiency as _raw_global_efficiency,
+    local_efficiency as _raw_local_efficiency,
 )
 
+
+def efficiency(G, u, v):
+    """br-isokw: ``G`` matches nx; Rust binding used ``g``."""
+    return _raw_efficiency(G, u, v)
+
+
+def global_efficiency(G):
+    """br-isokw: ``G`` matches nx."""
+    return _raw_global_efficiency(G)
+
+
+def local_efficiency(G):
+    """br-isokw: ``G`` matches nx."""
+    return _raw_local_efficiency(G)
+
 # Algorithm functions — broadcasting

     tree_broadcast_center,
